articles.title: Stocks Rise Toward Record as Iran Peace Hopes Hold: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Wall Street kicked off the holiday-shortened week with stocks joining bonds higher as hopes for a peace deal between the US and Iran overshadowed military strikes in the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: Gold Declines as Gulf Strikes Cloud Interest Rate Outlook
articles.summary: Gold fell as military strikes in the Persian Gulf dimmed hopes for a US-Iran peace deal and reinforced concerns that persistent inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

articles.title: Oil Rises as US-Iran Clashes Muddy Outlook for Peace Deal
articles.summary: Oil rebounded as fresh clashes between the US and Iran clouded the outlook for an interim deal to reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US Bonds Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Iran Progress
articles.summary: Treasuries rose as investors reacted to oil price declines after US President Donald Trump said peace negotiations with Iran were proceeding, even as tensions in the Middle East remain high.

articles.title: US Consumer Confidence Slips Amid Widespread Price Worries
articles.summary: US consumer confidence edged down in May as views of current economic conditions settled back amid rising prices due to the war in Iran.
